message:
feat(testing): add ext_release_binary_e2e tool for live-provider extension validation

New binary (src/bin/ext_release_binary_e2e.rs, 922 lines) that validates
extension loading and basic operation against a release-built pi binary
using a live provider endpoint. This fills the gap between unit/conformance
tests (which use mocked providers) and real-world usage.

Purpose:
- Spawns the actual release pi binary for each extension in the validated
  manifest, verifying that the extension loads, the provider responds, and
  pi exits cleanly
- Designed for CI release gates and local pre-release validation
- Supports sharded parallel execution for large extension corpora

CLI interface (clap-derived):
- --manifest: path to VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json (default: tests/ext_conformance/)
- --artifacts-root: extension artifact directory
- --pi-bin: path to release binary under test (default: target/release/pi)
- --provider/--model/--api-key: provider configuration (default: ollama)
- --prompt: test prompt sent to each extension run
- --timeout-secs: per-extension timeout (default: 90s)
- --jobs: concurrency level for parallel execution
- --allow-dangerous: set PI_EXTENSION_ALLOW_DANGEROUS=1
- --extension-policy: optional policy override (safe/balanced/permissive)
- --shard-index/--shard-total: partition for distributed runs
- --max-cases: optional cap on selected extensions
- --report: output JSON report path

Execution model:
- Reads validated manifest, filters to extensions with "pass" status
- Optional sharding partitions the extension list deterministically
- Thread pool (std::thread + mpsc) runs --jobs cases concurrently
- Each case: spawns pi with --print mode, extension entry, provider flags
- Captures stdout/stderr, exit code, wall time
- Classifies result as pass/fail/timeout with failure reason

Output:
- JSON report with per-extension results, timing, and summary statistics
- Exit code 0 if all cases pass, 1 if any fail

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
